target.name: Cerebras
target.founder: Andrew Feldman, Gary Lauterbach, Michael James, Sean Lie, Jean-Philippe Fricker

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Cerebras is a public-stage semiconductor company founded by Andrew Feldman and four co-founders. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public peers in Chips & Semiconductors (Credo Semiconductor at $5.0B, Ouster at $1.5B, and Aeva Technologies at $1.0B), supplemented by 8 private frontier-tech players including PsiQuantum and Etched.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The median valuation across the 3 same-sector, same-stage peers is $1.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $5.0B. A public-comp anchoring method using sector P/S multiples (median 20.3x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD) cannot be applied because Cerebras's revenue is not disclosed. Given the single applicable method, triangulation yields a narrow implied range of $1.3B to $1.7B (median $1.5B).

The current market valuation of $23B sits substantially above this peer-derived range—a 15-fold premium to the median. This divergence likely reflects market expectations around Cerebras's technology differentiation, total addressable market potential, or scale trajectories relative to the sampled peers. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, gross margins relative to semiconductor sector norms, and whether the company's technical approach commands a valuation premium justified by forward fundamentals. Additional disclosure of financial metrics would enable revenue- or earnings-based methods to narrow the range.
